content: Whose son was Joel?
explanation: Joel 1:1 — 'The word of the LORD that came to Joel, the son of Pethuel.' This is the only biographical detail about the author in the entire book.

content: According to Joel 1:4, what great disaster fell on the land?
explanation: Joel 1:4 — 'What the cutting locust left, the swarming locust has eaten. What the swarming locust left, the hopping locust has eaten, and what the hopping locust left, the destroying locust has eaten.' Successive swarms of locusts devour the crops — a picture of devastating judgment.
options: Successive swarms of locusts that devoured everything

content: In Joel 1:14, what does the prophet call the people to do?
explanation: Joel 1:14 — 'Consecrate a fast; call a solemn assembly. Gather the elders and all the inhabitants of the land to the house of the LORD your God, and cry out to the LORD.'
options: Raise an army against the locusts
Offer abundant sacrifices to bring rain
Consecrate a fast, call a solemn assembly, and cry to the LORD
Migrate to another land to escape famine

content: What does Joel 1:15 declare is near?
explanation: Joel 1:15 — 'Alas for the day! For the day of the LORD is near, and as destruction from the Almighty it comes.'

content: According to Joel 2:1, what does blowing the trumpet in Zion announce?
explanation: Joel 2:1 — 'Blow a trumpet in Zion; sound an alarm on my holy mountain! Let all the inhabitants of the land tremble, for the day of the LORD is coming; it is near.'
options: The Day of the LORD is near
The Passover is about to begin

content: In Joel 2:12, how does the LORD call His people to return to Him?
explanation: Joel 2:12 — 'Yet even now, declares the LORD, return to me with all your heart, with fasting, with weeping, and with mourning.' True return must come from the heart, expressed in fasting, weeping, and mourning.
options: With all the heart, with fasting, weeping, and mourning

content: According to Joel 2:13, what should the people rend to show true repentance?
explanation: Joel 2:13 — 'Rend your hearts and not your garments. Return to the LORD your God...' Rending garments is an outward sign; God desires true brokenness of heart.
options: Outer garment for all to see

content: How does Joel 2:13 describe the LORD's character?
explanation: Joel 2:13 — '...for he is gracious and merciful, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love; and he relents over disaster.' A classic description of God's character, echoing Exodus 34:6.
options: Slow to speak but quick to punish
Full of glory and absolute power
Gracious and merciful, slow to anger, abounding in steadfast love

content: In Joel 2:25, what does God promise to restore?
explanation: Joel 2:25 — 'I will restore to you the years that the swarming locust has eaten, the hopper, the destroyer, and the cutter, my great army, which I sent among you.' A promise of reversal — the very locusts that judged are repaid by God.
options: The years the locust has eaten

content: According to Joel 2:28, upon whom will God pour out His Spirit?
explanation: Joel 2:28 — 'And it shall come to pass afterward, that I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh; your sons and your daughters shall prophesy...' Peter quotes this at Pentecost (Acts 2:17).

content: According to Joel 2:28, when the Spirit is poured out, what will young men do and old men do?
explanation: Joel 2:28 — '...your old men shall dream dreams, and your young men shall see visions.' Both old and young receive revelation by the Spirit.
options: Young men dream dreams, old men see visions
Young men labor, old men pray
Young men see visions, old men dream dreams
Young men write books, old men teach

content: According to Joel 2:32, who shall be saved?
explanation: Joel 2:32 — 'And it shall come to pass that everyone who calls on the name of the LORD shall be saved...' Paul quotes this in Romans 10:13 — calling on the name of the LORD is the path of salvation.
options: Everyone who calls on the name of the LORD

content: What does Joel 3:10 command the nations to do with their plowshares and pruning hooks?
explanation: Joel 3:10 — 'Beat your plowshares into swords, and your pruning hooks into spears...' This reverses Isaiah 2:4 — in the day of judgment on the nations, peaceful tools are turned into weapons.
options: Beat them into swords and spears
Offer them upon the altar of the LORD
Bury them in the earth as treasure

content: In Joel 3:14, 'multitudes, multitudes' are gathered in what valley?
explanation: Joel 3:14 — 'Multitudes, multitudes, in the valley of decision! For the day of the LORD is near in the valley of decision.' The Valley of Decision is an image of the place where the LORD judges the nations.
options: The Valley of Jehoshaphat of peace
The Valley of Hinnom of fire
The Valley of Achor of shame

content: According to Joel 3:17, when the LORD dwells in Zion, what will Jerusalem be?
explanation: Joel 3:17 — '...So you shall know that I am the LORD your God, who dwells in Zion, my holy mountain. And Jerusalem shall be holy, and strangers shall never again pass through it.'
options: Holy, and strangers shall never again pass through

content: According to Joel 3:18, what will drip from the mountains on the day of restoration?
explanation: Joel 3:18 — 'And in that day the mountains shall drip sweet wine, and the hills shall flow with milk, and all the streambeds of Judah shall flow with water...' An image of lavish abundance in the time of restoration.

content: According to Joel 2:23, which two rains does God give?
explanation: Joel 2:23 — '...he has given the early rain for your vindication; he has poured down for you abundant rain, the early and the latter rain, as before.' The early rain (Oct–Nov) enables sowing; the latter rain (Mar–Apr) ripens the harvest.
options: The early rain and the latter rain

content: According to Joel 2:13, which attributes describe the LORD? (select all that apply)
explanation: Joel 2:13 — '...for he is gracious and merciful, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love; and he relents over disaster.' 'Hard-hearted and unyielding' is not part of God's nature as described.
options: Abounding in steadfast love, relenting over disaster

content: According to Joel 2:28-29, upon which groups will God pour out His Spirit? (select all that apply)
explanation: Joel 2:28-29 — 'Your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams, and your young men shall see visions. Even on the male and female servants in those days I will pour out my Spirit.' Angels are not the recipients of the Spirit.

content: According to Joel 3:18, on the day of restoration, what will flow from the land? (select all that apply)
explanation: Joel 3:18 — 'The mountains shall drip sweet wine, and the hills shall flow with milk, and all the streambeds of Judah shall flow with water...' Three images of abundance; 'rain of fire' does not appear in this verse.
options: Water from all the streambeds of Judah
Rain of fire from the heavens
